mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the effective federal funds rate raise risk-free discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, reducing present equity values and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day SPX returns is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate that rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window reflects deteriorating household income and consumption demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate and the unemployment rate reflect a restrictive monetary policy that is cooling labor demand, a combination that tightens financial conditions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS and UNRATE both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression has a positive coefficient (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated, month-over-month decreases in industrial production reflect supply-side constraints that squeeze corporate margins and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is absent in low-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL > 300, declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contractions in industrial production reflect immediate negative surprises in manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not predict significantly negative 5-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts reflect a broad-based demand collapse that reduces expected corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint decline condition fails to produce negative average 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, further month-over-month increases in the policy rate push real borrowing costs into restrictive territory, producing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS > 5%, additional rate increases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in housing starts reflect weakening construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to lower aggregate output expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Declines in HOUST are not associated with significantly negative 60-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.
